If discussing treatment despite L1 scope, mentions desmopressin as standard therapy for central DI and emphasizes monitoring to avoid hyponatremia/water intoxication.

If discussing treatment despite L1 scope, performs required safety checks before prescribing: allergy_check (non-critical) and both contraindication_check and drug_interaction_check (critical) prior to any desmopressin order.
Communicates a basic follow-up/monitoring concept (symptom tracking, electrolytes/sodium monitoring if treated) and provides clear return precautions for worsening symptoms.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose central diabetes insipidus based on classic history and basic evaluation, provide explanation and safety-oriented guidance.
must_collect: signs/symptoms of dehydration (dizziness, dry mouth, weight loss)
history of pituitary/hypothalamic disease, tumors, infiltrative disease
basic exclusion of diabetes mellitus symptoms/history (hyperglycemia, known DM)
steps: Elicit history focused on water balance symptoms and potential central causes (head injury, pituitary disease) and key differentials (DM, nephrogenic DI, primary polydipsia).
Assess for urgent red flags (hemodynamic instability, severe dehydration, altered mental status, severe headache/visual symptoms) and direct to urgent care/ED if present.
Explain diagnosis milestone: explicitly state 'central diabetes insipidus' and connect to 'polyuria' from low ADH/vasopressin.

Discuss general management principles (maintain hydration; avoid unsupervised fluid restriction; outline that desmopressin is typical therapy and requires monitoring for hyponatremia if started by a clinician).
Record diagnosis using record_diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Diagnosis-first with explicit uncertainty but still records central DI as working diagnosis after excluding obvious diabetes mellitus and noting classic features; advises confirmatory testing and safety precautions.
steps: Gather classic symptoms (polyuria/polydipsia/nocturia) and rule out obvious hyperglycemia symptoms/history.

State working diagnosis as central diabetes insipidus and briefly mention need for confirmatory evaluation (e.g., serum sodium/osmolality and urine osmolality; supervised water deprivation test when appropriate).
Provide return precautions and hydration guidance.
Record diagnosis using record_diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Differential-aware approach: discusses central DI vs nephrogenic DI vs primary polydipsia, but concludes central DI is most likely and records it.
steps: Elicit medication history (lithium) and renal history to consider nephrogenic DI; assess psychiatric history/excess water intake for primary polydipsia.
Explain why central diabetes insipidus is favored (e.g., central risk factors, symptom pattern) while acknowledging alternatives.
Explain diagnosis milestone including 'central diabetes insipidus' and 'polyuria'.
Record diagnosis using record_diagnosis.
